paper_title: Molecular identification of Ralstonia pseudosolanacearum causing bacterial wilt in tomatoes from Da Nang by using Colony PCR

entities.description: The main field of research involving molecular techniques to identify plant pathogens.
A serious vascular disease in plants caused by the Ralstonia solanacearum species complex.
A group of pathogenic bacteria causing wilt in many agricultural crops.
A specific genospecies within the RSSC, often associated with phylotypes I and III.
A genetic classification within RSSC primarily of Asian origin.
A method to amplify DNA directly from bacterial colonies without prior DNA extraction.
A PCR technique using multiple primer sets to identify different phylotypes simultaneously.
Molecular technique used for the identification and phylogenetic analysis of bacteria.
A selective culture medium used for isolating Ralstonia solanacearum.
The study of evolutionary relationships among biological entities using genetic data.
A publicly available database of nucleotide sequences.
The specific acidity level of the TZC medium used in the experiment.
The success rate of the colony PCR technique applied to the subcolonies.
The confidence level measured for the phylotype I clade in the phylogenetic tree.
Lead author affiliated with the University of Science and Education.
Co-author affiliated with the University of Science and Education.

entities.description: The academic institution where the authors are affiliated.
